text:
This research investigates the effectiveness of utilizing the textbook "Al-Hilal Fi Fann Al-Lughah Al-Arabiya" to improve the speaking skills (Mahārah al-Kalām) of students at the Ar Riyadh Islamic Boarding School in Palembang. The study was prompted by the observation that the student body is highly heterogeneous—including graduates from non-religious schools and professional employees—while existing instructional materials often relied on archaic or uncommon vocabulary that hindered daily conversational fluency.

Adopting a quantitative experimental methodology, the study involved a sample of students from class 1 "C." Participants were divided into an experimental group, which used the Al-Hilal textbook, and a control group, which followed conventional teaching methods. Data were gathered through observation, interviews, questionnaires, and a pre-test/post-test design. 

Statistical analysis using the t-test revealed a significant improvement in the experimental group, which achieved a t-value of 5.38, surpassing the critical table values at both the 5% and 1% significance levels. In contrast, the control group showed less substantial progress. Qualitative findings from interviews and observations further supported these results, indicating that 86% of students preferred the new material due to its relevant contemporary phrases and practical dialogue structures. The study concludes that "Al-Hilal Fi Fann Al-Lughah Al-Arabiya" is a highly effective tool for developing Arabic speaking proficiency, as it better aligns with the students' communicative needs compared to traditional textbooks.

**Keywords:** Arabic Pedagogy, Speaking Skills, Al-Hilal Textbook, Islamic Boarding School.
